title: lomatium rigidum
intro: Lomatium rigidum, also known as meadow brome or biscuitroot, is a flowering plant native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds, which are found in its roots and seeds. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and pneumonia, and skin conditions, such as acne and eczema. Lomatium rigidum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or poultice, and has been used by herbalists for centuries due to its health benefits and versatility in herbal medicine.
